questions.question: What does `typeof null` return in JavaScript?
Which method creates a shallow copy of an array?
What is the output of `0.1 + 0.2 === 0.3` in JavaScript?
Which keyword declares a block-scoped variable?
What does the `Promise.all()` method do?
options: Resolves when the first promise resolves

questions.question: What is a closure in JavaScript?
options: A function with no return value
A function that retains access to its outer scope after the outer function has returned
A method that closes the browser window

questions.question: Which array method returns a new array with elements that pass a test?
What does the spread operator `...` do when used with an array?
options: Expands the array into individual elements

questions.question: What is event delegation?
options: Attaching an event listener to each child element
Attaching a single event listener to a parent to handle events from children

questions.question: Which statement correctly describes `async/await`?
options: It is syntactic sugar over Promises
It makes code run in parallel by default

questions.question: What hook is used to manage local component state?
When does `useEffect` with an empty dependency array `[]` run?
What is the purpose of the `key` prop in a list?
options: To help React identify which items changed

questions.question: What does `React.memo` do?
options: Memoizes the return value of a function
Prevents a component from re-rendering if props haven't changed

questions.question: Which hook lets you access a DOM element directly?
What is prop drilling?
options: Passing props through many nested components that don't need them

questions.question: What does the Context API solve?
options: Prop drilling by providing a way to share values without passing props

questions.question: What is a controlled component?
options: A component whose form data is handled by React state

questions.question: What does `useCallback` return?
In React, what triggers a re-render?
options: Calling any function inside the component

questions.question: What is the difference between `interface` and `type` in TypeScript?
options: Interfaces can be extended and merged; types are more flexible with unions
Types support classes; interfaces do not

questions.question: What does the `unknown` type represent?
options: A value that is always undefined
A type-safe counterpart to `any` that requires type checking before use

questions.question: What is a generic in TypeScript?
options: A type that can only be a string or number
A placeholder type that is specified when the function or class is used

questions.question: What does `Partial<T>` do?
options: Makes all properties of T required
Makes all properties of T optional
Picks a subset of T's properties

questions.question: What is a union type?
options: A type that combines two interfaces
A type that can be one of several types
A type that must satisfy all listed types

questions.question: What does the `readonly` modifier do?
options: Prevents a property from being reassigned after initialization
Converts a property to a getter

questions.question: What is a type guard?
options: A way to prevent runtime errors
A runtime check that narrows a type within a conditional block

questions.question: What does `keyof T` produce?
options: A union of the keys of T

questions.question: What is the `never` type used for?
options: Functions that never return (throw or infinite loop)

questions.question: What does `as const` do to an object literal?
options: Infers the narrowest literal types for all properties

questions.question: What is the event loop in Node.js?
options: A loop that iterates over arrays
A mechanism that handles asynchronous callbacks in a non-blocking way

questions.question: Which module is used to create an HTTP server in Node.js without frameworks?
What does `process.env` provide?
What is the purpose of `package.json`?
options: To define project metadata, dependencies, and scripts

questions.question: What does `require()` do in Node.js?
options: Loads and caches a CommonJS module

questions.question: What is middleware in Express.js?
options: A function that has access to req, res, and next in the request-response cycle

questions.question: What does `fs.readFile` do by default?
options: Reads a file asynchronously and calls a callback

questions.question: What is a stream in Node.js?
options: An object that lets you read or write data piece by piece

questions.question: What does `npm install --save-dev` do?
options: Installs a package as a development dependency

questions.question: What is the purpose of `cluster` module in Node.js?
options: To spawn child processes that share the same server port for load balancing

questions.question: What is a smart contract?
options: A legal document stored on a server
Self-executing code deployed on a blockchain

questions.question: What does the `payable` modifier do in Solidity?
options: Allows a function to modify state
Allows a function to receive Ether

questions.question: What is a mapping in Solidity?
options: A key-value store similar to a hash map

questions.question: What does `msg.sender` refer to?
options: The address that called the current function

questions.question: What is a reentrancy attack?
options: Calling a contract function twice in the same transaction
An exploit where a malicious contract repeatedly calls back into the victim contract before state is updated

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `emit` keyword?
options: To trigger an event and log it on the blockchain

questions.question: What does `view` mean on a function?
options: The function reads state but does not modify it

questions.question: What is an ABI in the context of Solidity?
options: A binary encoding of the contract bytecode
A JSON description of a contract's functions and events used to interact with it

questions.question: What does `require()` do in Solidity?
options: Validates a condition and reverts the transaction if it fails

questions.question: What is the difference between `storage` and `memory` in Solidity?
options: Storage is temporary; memory is permanent
Storage is persistent on-chain; memory is temporary during function execution

questions.question: What is the native asset on the Stellar network?
What is SEP-0010 used for?
What is a Soroban smart contract written in?
What is the minimum account balance required on Stellar?
What does Horizon provide?
options: An HTTP API to interact with the Stellar network

questions.question: What is a trustline on Stellar?
options: An account's opt-in to hold a specific non-native asset

questions.question: What is the Freighter wallet used for?
options: A browser extension wallet for signing Stellar transactions

questions.question: What does `invoke_contract` do in Soroban?
options: Calls a function on an already-deployed Soroban contract

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Stellar Consensus Protocol (SCP)?
options: To achieve network consensus without proof-of-work via federated Byzantine agreement

questions.question: What is a claimable balance on Stellar?
options: An asset amount set aside that specific accounts can claim under defined conditions
